package.name: Ultimate East Java Experience: 5D4N Ijen Crater, Tumpak Sewu & Bromo
package.overview:
Embark on an exciting 5-day journey through East Java's most iconic landscapes. This adventure is perfect for nature enthusiasts and explorers eager to witness breathtaking sights.
Begin with a trek to Ijen Crater to witness the rare blue flames and the stunning turquoise crater lake at sunrise. Next, relax at the pristine shores of Papuma   Beach, known for its white sands and clear waters. The journey continues to the majestic Tumpak  Sewu Waterfall, a multi-tiered waterfall surrounded by lush greenery. Finally, experience the mesmerizing sunrise over Mount Bromo, illuminating the vast caldera and surrounding peaks.
  
  
Throughout the tour, enjoy comfortable accommodations, private transportation, and the guidance of experienced English-speaking guides. This expedition is designed to provide a seamless and enriching exploration of Java's natural wonders, ensuring an unforgettable experience for all participants.

galleries.alt_text: Stunning sunrise over Mount Bromo with panoramic views of the Tengger Caldera and surrounding mountains

destinations.name: Surabaya
destinations.description: The city of Surabaya (Hanacaraka: ꦏꦹꦛꦯꦹꦫꦨꦪ; Javanese Pegon: اسورابايا, tr. Kutha Surabaya, Javanese pronunciation: [kuʈɔ surɔˈbɔjɔ]. Madura Pegon: , tr. Sorbhâjâh. Chinese: ) is the capital city of East Java Province, Indonesia, as well as a city largest metropolitan area in the province. Surabaya is the second largest city in Indonesia after Jakarta.

destinations.name: Mount Ijen
destinations.description: Mount Ijen is a volcano located on the border of Banyuwangi Regency and Bondowoso Regency, East Java, Indonesia. This mountain has a height of 2,386 meters above sea level and is located side by side with Mount Merapi. Mount Ijen last erupted in 1999. One of the most famous natural phenomena of Mount Ijen is the blue fire.
destinations.weather_by_season: Dry Season (May–October): Ideal for trekking, with cooler temperatures (10°C–20°C or 50°F–68°F) and clear skies.Wet Season (November–April): Warmer (12°C–22°C or 54°F–72°F), higher humidity, and frequent rain.
destinations.rainfall_intensity: Heaviest Rainfall: December–February.Least Rainfall: May–October. Rain during the wet season can make trails muddy and slippery.
destinations.trail_details: Trail Length: ~6 kilometers (3.7 miles) round trip.Ascent Duration: ~1.5–2 hours to the crater rim.Descent to Crater: ~30 minutes to the blue fire site (optional, challenging and rocky).
destinations.required_gear: Clothing: Warm layers (thermal, fleece, waterproof jacket).Footwear: Sturdy trekking boots with good grip.Protection: Gas mask for sulfur fumes, headlamp/flashlight.Other: Water, snacks.
destinations.difficulty_level: Moderate: Steep incline for the first 2 km, rocky descent into the crater (optional) increases difficulty. Suitable for individuals with trekking experience or moderate fitness levels.
destinations.environmental_factors: Altitude: Crater rim at ~2,386 m (7,828 ft), Paltuding at ~1,850 m (6,070 ft).Temperature: Night/early morning: 2°C–10°C (36°F–50°F). Daytime: 15°C–20°C (59°F–68°F).Wind Speed: Moderate, 10–20 km/h.
destinations.physical_requirements: Fitness Level: Moderate; ability to hike steep trails for 3–4 hours.Health Concerns: Not suitable for individuals with asthma or severe respiratory issues due to sulfur fumes.
destinations.main_attractions: Turquoise Crater Lake: World’s largest highly acidic lake (pH < 0.5).Blue Fire: Rare phenomenon.Sunrise Views: Over the volcanic landscape, including Mount Raung in the distance.
destinations.best_time_to_visit: Season: May–October (dry season) for clear skies and safer trails.Time of Day: Midnight to early morning for blue fire and sunrise.
destinations.tips_for_visitors: Start Early: Begin the trek by 2:00 AM.Pack Essentials: Gas mask, warm clothes, and waterproof gear (for wet season).

destinations.name: Papuma Beach
destinations.description: Papuma Beach is a beach that has become a tourist spot in Jember Regency, East Java Province, Indonesia. The name Papuma itself is actually an abbreviation of "Malikan White Sand". Papuma Beach is located in Lojejer Village, Wuluhan District, Jember Regency . Papuma beach is one of the most popular and well-known beaches in Jember.
destinations.weather_by_season: Dry Season (May–October): Clear skies, temperatures 24°C–32°C (75°F–89°F), ideal for outdoor activities.Wet Season (November–April): Occasional rain, warmer temperatures.
destinations.trail_details: Trail Options: Beach walks and short hikes to Siti Hinggil Hill.Duration: ~20–30 minutes to explore viewpoints or 1–2 hours for a full tour.
destinations.required_gear: Clothing: Lightweight and breathable clothes.Footwear: Sandals or water shoes.Other: Sunscreen, hat, and water.
destinations.difficulty_level: Easy: Suitable for visitors of all ages, with well-maintained paths and no steep inclines.
destinations.environmental_factors: Temperature: 24°C–32°C (75°F–89°F).Wind Speed: Light to moderate (5–15 km/h).Humidity: Moderate to high, especially during the wet season.
destinations.physical_requirements: Fitness Level: Minimal fitness required; suitable for families and older adults.Health Concerns: None.
destinations.main_attractions: Karst Rock Formations: Stunning limestone cliffs rising from the sea.Siti Hinggil Hill: Offers panoramic views of the beach.Crystal Clear Water: Ideal for photos and relaxation.
destinations.best_time_to_visit: Season: May–October.Time of Day: Morning or late afternoon for cooler weather and sunset views.
destinations.tips_for_visitors: Bring cash for local food stalls.Avoid swimming during high tide.

destinations.name: Tumpak Sewu Waterfall
destinations.description: Tumpak Sewu Waterfall or also called Coban Sewu is a waterfall with a height of about 120 meters. This waterfall is bordered by Lumajang Regency and Malang Regency, East Java Province. Tumpak Sewu Waterfall has a water flow that resembles a curtain so it is included in the Tiered waterfall type.
destinations.weather_by_season: Dry Season (May–October): Ideal for visits with clear skies and cooler conditions (22°C–30°C or 72°F–86°F).Wet Season (November–April): Frequent rains, slippery trails, and higher humidity.
destinations.rainfall_intensity: Heaviest Rainfall: December–February.Least Rainfall: May–October, providing safer and more enjoyable trekking conditions.
destinations.trail_details: Trail Length: ~1 km (0.6 miles) round trip.Duration: ~30–45 minutes to descend to the base.Challenges: Slippery paths, bamboo ladders, and rocky terrain.
destinations.required_gear: Clothing: Quick-dry clothes and waterproof layers.Footwear: Non-slip trekking sandals or water shoes.Other: Poncho, waterproof bag, walking poles, snacks, and water.
destinations.difficulty_level: Moderate: Requires descending steep paths and navigating wet, rocky sections.
destinations.environmental_factors: Altitude: Base of the waterfall at ~400 m (1,312 ft) above sea level.Temperature: Cool and refreshing (20°C–25°C or 68°F–77°F).Humidity: High (~80%–90%), with constant mist.
destinations.physical_requirements: Fitness Level: Moderate; suitable for individuals comfortable with uneven terrain and water crossings.Health Concerns: Avoid during or after heavy rain due to the risk of flash floods.
destinations.main_attractions: Horseshoe-Shaped Waterfall: Cascading water creates a mesmerizing view.
destinations.best_time_to_visit: Season: May–October (dry season).Time of Day: Morning for cooler temperatures and better lighting.
destinations.tips_for_visitors: Wear waterproof gear and expect to get wet.Avoid the trek during or after heavy rain.

destinations.name: Mount Bromo
destinations.description: Mount Bromo or in Tenggerese spelled "Brama", also called the Tengger Caldera, is an active volcano in East Java, Indonesia. This mountain has a height of 2,329 meters above sea level and is located in four districts, namely Probolinggo Regency, Pasuruan Regency, Lumajang Regency, and Malang Regency.
destinations.weather_by_season: Dry Season (May–October): Cooler temperatures (5°C–20°C or 41°F–68°F) and clear skies.Wet Season (November–April): Frequent rain, warmer temperatures (10°C–25°C or 50°F–77°F).
destinations.rainfall_intensity: Heaviest Rainfall: December–February.Least Rainfall: May–October, ideal for sunrise views and safer treks.
destinations.trail_details: Jeep Ride: Access viewpoints like Kingkong Hill.Trek: ~2 km trek to the crater involves climbing 250+ steps.Sea of Sand: Volcanic sand surrounds the area.
destinations.required_gear: Clothing: Warm layers, thermal jacket.Footwear: Sturdy trekking boots.Other: Flashlight, sunscreen, dust mask.
destinations.difficulty_level: Easy to Moderate: Jeep rides minimize effort; optional trekking to the crater rim adds physical demand.
destinations.environmental_factors: Altitude: ~2,329 m (7,641 ft).Temperature: Night: 5°C–10°C (41°F–50°F); daytime: 15°C–20°C (59°F–68°F).Wind Speed: Moderate (10–20 km/h).
destinations.physical_requirements: Fitness Level: Minimal to moderate; suitable for most visitors.Health Concerns: Dust can irritate sensitive lungs—carry a mask if needed.
destinations.main_attractions: Sunrise Views: Spectacular vistas of Mount Bromo and Mount Semeru.Bromo Crater: Active crater with a surreal landscape.Teletubbies Hill: Lush green savanna nearby.
destinations.best_time_to_visit: Season: May–October.Time of Day: Early morning for sunrise and cooler temperatures.
destinations.tips_for_visitors: Dress warmly for early morning treks.

itinerary.title: Arrival in Surabaya and Journey to Bondowoso
itinerary.details:
Arrival in Surabaya and Scenic Transfer to Bondowoso
Your exciting Bromo and Ijen adventure begins with a warm welcome at Surabaya Airport. We’ll pick you up (best before noon) and start our scenic 5-hour drive to Bondowoso. Along the way, we’ll stop at a local restaurant in Probolinggo for lunch at your own expense. Enjoy the changing landscapes as you make your way towards Bondowoso.
#next-stop#
Arrival at Grand Padis Hotel
After arriving at Grand Padis Hotel in Bondowoso, take some time to relax and settle in. Enjoy a delicious dinner and rest up for the next day’s adventure.

itinerary.title: Ijen Crater Adventure and Papuma Beach Sunset
itinerary.details:
Ijen Crater Exploration
Your day starts early at midnight with a drive to Paltuding, the base of Ijen Crater. After about 1.5 hours on the road, you’ll begin your 1-1.5 hour trek to the crater rim. Upon reaching the rim, you’ll be greeted by the breathtaking sight of the 200-meter deep turquoise sulfuric lake, set against a dramatic volcanic landscape. Witness the extraordinary work of sulfur miners who carry heavy loads through hazardous terrain and toxic fumes.
#next-stop#
Coffee Break at Malabar Coffee Plantation
After enjoying the sunrise at Ijen Crater, we stop at the Malabar Coffee Plantation for a refreshing coffee break and snacks. Then, we return to your hotel in Bondowoso for a shower and check-out before heading to the next destination.
#next-stop#
Papuma Beach Sunset
In the afternoon, we journey to Jember City, where you’ll check in at Doho Homestay. After a brief rest, we set off for Papuma Beach, a one-hour drive away. Be mesmerized by the stunning sunset views over the sea, offering the perfect end to a day filled with natural wonders.

itinerary.title: Tumpak Sewu Waterfall and Journey to Bromo
itinerary.details:
Tumpak Sewu Waterfall: The Thousand Waterfalls
Our journey begins early at 4:00 AM as we check out and head to Tumpak Sewu Waterfall, one of the most beautiful waterfalls in Java. Known for its unique half-circle formation, the waterfall consists of multiple mini-waterfalls cascading from a height of around 300 meters. A short, adventurous trek takes you across rocks and streams to reach the waterfall, so be sure to bring appropriate trekking sandals or shoes.
#next-stop#
Lunch and Journey to Bromo
After exploring the falls and perhaps even enjoying a natural water spa, we will have lunch before continuing our journey to the Bromo area. The drive will take approximately 4 hours, bringing you closer to the iconic Bromo Volcano.

itinerary.title: Bromo Sunrise Adventure and Return to Surabaya
itinerary.details:
Bromo Stargazing and Sunrise
Prepare for an early morning adventure starting at 2:00 AM as we set out for stargazing and a Milky Way view at Mount Bromo. As the first rays of dawn break, witness the spectacular sunrise over the Bromo landscape. The majestic views of Mount Batok, Mount Bromo, and Mount Semeru will take your breath away, as they rise from the flat volcanic sand bed of the caldera.
#next-stop#
Exploring the Heart of Bromo
After sunrise, we will continue our journey on a thrilling 4WD ride over the vast Sand Sea to the Poten Temple. From here, you can either take a 3km hike or a pony ride to the base of Mount Bromo, where you will ascend 253 steps to reach the crater rim and witness the raw power of the volcano up close.
#next-stop#
Return to Surabaya
After a morning filled with adventure, we continue our journey to Surabaya for an overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Departure from Surabaya
itinerary.details:
Farewell to East Java
After breakfast, you will be transferred to Juanda International Airport for your flight back home, reflecting on
  your amazing adventure in East Java.

time.08:00-13:00: Pickup from Surabaya and scenic drive to Bondowoso.

time.17:00: Check-in at Grand Padis Hotel, Bondowoso.

time.02:00: Blue fire/sunrise trek at Ijen Crater.

time.08:00: Breakfast and refresh at Malabar Coffee Plantation.

time.14:00: Check in at Doho Homestay, Jember.

time.07:00: Guided trek to Tumpak Sewu Waterfall.

time.15:00: Check-in at Holiday Inn Express Surabaya.

time.19:00: Optional evening stroll at Surabaya city center.

time.10:00: Check-out and transfer to Juanda International Airport.
